questions.question: What is the main pathophysiological mechanism of polymorphic ventricular tachycardia in acute myocardial ischemia?
options: Functional reentry due to heterogeneity of the ischemic substrate
questions.explanation: In acute myocardial ischemia, electrical heterogeneity between ischemic and nonischemic zones creates a substrate for functional reentry, which is the most common mechanism of polymorphic ventricular tachycardia.

questions.question: What electrocardiographic finding is characteristic of polymorphic ventricular tachycardia associated with myocardial ischemia?
options: Wide QRS complexes with variable morphology
Constant electrical axis and inverted T waves
Negative P waves in inferior leads
questions.explanation: Polymorphic ventricular tachycardia is characterized by wide QRS complexes that vary in morphology, amplitude, and beat-to-beat direction, reflecting the instability of the ischemic substrate.

questions.question: What is the first-line treatment for unstable polymorphic ventricular tachycardia in the setting of acute myocardial ischemia?
questions.explanation: In unstable polymorphic ventricular tachycardia, especially if it is polymorphic, immediate non-synchronized defibrillation (asynchronous shock) is recommended to restore sinus rhythm, since synchronized cardioversion may not be effective.

questions.question: What ionic alteration directly contributes to the appearance of polymorphic ventricular tachycardia during myocardial ischemia?
questions.explanation: Acute ischemia produces potassium release into the extracellular space, generating local hyperkalemia that partially depolarizes myocardial cells, favoring functional reentry and polymorphic ventricular tachycardia.

questions.question: What is the key difference between polymorphic ventricular tachycardia and ventricular fibrillation on electrocardiogram?
options: Ventricular fibrillation has visible P waves
Ventricular fibrillation has a regular rhythm
The polymorphic one lacks electrical activity
questions.explanation: In polymorphic ventricular tachycardia the QRS complexes are wide and organized although variable, while ventricular fibrillation shows chaotic, disorganized electrical activity without defined QRS complexes.

questions.question: What factor most frequently triggers polymorphic ventricular tachycardia in acute coronary syndrome?
questions.explanation: Acute transmural myocardial ischemia, typically due to complete coronary occlusion, diffusely alters the electrical substrate, generating the conditions for functional reentry and polymorphic ventricular tachycardia.
hashtags: ESC 2020 guideline for acute coronary syndrome
